data.body: HANDOFF / WALL. Epic bbf1 (doctorial-semiironic-wrasse): 5 stories + 4af8 all MERGED+CLOSED (S1 ef33 change900 / S2 ce4d 901 / S3 94f9 902 / S4 6673 903 / S5 dfb9 905 / 4af8 914). Also merged: 5e94 flaky-perf fix (911). Filed process bugs: 80af (docs/discoverability: close-time completion-verify target IS configurable via verify-completion --ref / REBAR_GATE_REF / [snapshot].ref but not surfaced on transition-close), relates 4636. EPIC STILL OPEN: its AC3 'zero adapters.jira imports in core' is NOT met. Attempted residual as ticket 97f2 (child of bbf1) = extend Backend port with field helpers -> Gerrit change 920 (CODE gates green LLM+1/Verified+1) BUT plan-review BLOCKED (T1): putting diff_fields/extract_field/assignee_matches (parameterized on jira_fields shapes) on the NEUTRAL OutboundMapper port violates ADR 0035 §(d).2 (core owns diff/apply; port only translates) and re-couples a future GitHub adapter. Advisory G1G2 also caught inbound_probe.py:65 as a 7th coupled core module I missed. 920 NOT submitted (won't force a red plan vote). THE WALL: the residual field-comparison helpers operate on the vendor snapshot shape; neither port nor core is a clean home. Correct fix = deeper redesign: convert fetched vendor snapshot to canonical/local via backend.inbound.map_remote_to_local BEFORE diffing, so outbound differ compares LOCAL-vs-LOCAL (no vendor-shaped comparison anywhere). That is a large reconciliation-core change. DECISION PENDING (user): (A) accept 4af8 delivered scope, narrow AC3, file the canonical-shape redesign as a proper story, close epic; (B) do the deep redesign now; (C) force 920 (not recommended). Worktrees: impl-bbf1-vendor-seam (S1-S5 merged), impl-4af8-differ-di (97f2 work = commit for change 920, uncommitted-clean otherwise), fix-overlap-perf-flake (5e94 merged).
